<skill_content name="tdd">
# Skill: tdd

# Test-Driven Development

TDD is the red → green loop. This skill is the reference that makes that loop produce tests worth keeping: what a good test is, where tests go, the anti-patterns, and the rules of the loop. Every section applies on every cycle — consult them before and during the loop, not after.

When exploring the codebase, read `CONTEXT.md` (if it exists) so test names and interface vocabulary match the project's domain language, and respect ADRs in the area you're touching.

## What a good test is

Tests verify behavior through public interfaces, not implementation details. Code can change entirely; tests shouldn't. A good test reads like a specification — "user can checkout with valid cart" tells you exactly what capability exists — and survives refactors because it doesn't care about internal structure.

See [tests.md](tests.md) for examples and [mocking.md](mocking.md) for mocking guidelines.

## Seams — where tests go

A **seam** is the public boundary you test at: the interface where you observe behavior without reaching inside. Tests live at seams, never against internals.

**Test only at pre-agreed seams.** Before writing any test, write down the seams under test and confirm them with the user. No test is written at an unconfirmed seam. You can't test everything — agreeing the seams up front is how testing effort lands on the critical paths and complex logic instead of every edge case.

Ask: "What's the public interface, and which seams should we test?"

## Anti-patterns

- **Implementation-coupled** — mocks internal collaborators, tests private methods, or verifies through a side channel (querying the database instead of using the interface). The tell: the test breaks when you refactor but behavior hasn't changed.
- **Tautological** — the assertion recomputes the expected value the way the code does (`expect(add(a, b)).toBe(a + b)`, a snapshot derived by hand the same way, a constant asserted equal to itself), so it passes by construction and can never disagree with the code. Expected values must come from an independent source of truth — a known-good literal, a worked example, the spec.
- **Horizontal slicing** — writing all tests first, then all implementation. Bulk tests verify _imagined_ behavior: you test the _shape_ of things rather than user-facing behavior, the tests go insensitive to real changes, and you commit to test structure before understanding the implementation. Work in **vertical slices** instead — one test → one implementation → repeat, each test a **tracer bullet** that responds to what the last cycle taught you.

## Rules of the loop

- **Red before green.** Write the failing test first, then only enough code to pass it. Don't anticipate future tests or add speculative features.
- **One slice at a time.** One seam, one test, one minimal implementation per cycle.
- **Refactoring is not part of the loop.** It belongs to the review stage (see the `code-review` skill), not the red → green implementation cycle.

1: # Issue tracker: GitHub
2: 
3: Issues and PRDs for this repo live as GitHub issues. Use the `gh` CLI for all operations.
4: 
5: ## Conventions
6: 
7: - **Create an issue**: `gh issue create --title "..." --body "..."`. Use a heredoc for multi-line bodies.
8: - **Read an issue**: `gh issue view <number> --comments`, filtering comments by `jq` and also fetching labels.
9: - **List issues**: `gh issue list --state open --json number,title,body,labels,comments --jq '[.[] | {number, title, body, labels: [.labels[].name], comments: [.comments[].body]}]'` with appropriate `--label` and `--state` filters.
10: - **Comment on an issue**: `gh issue comment <number> --body "..."`
11: - **Apply / remove labels**: `gh issue edit <number> --add-label "..."` / `--remove-label "..."`
12: - **Close**: `gh issue close <number> --comment "..."`
13: 
14: Infer the repo from `git remote -v` — `gh` does this automatically when run inside a clone.
15: 
16: ## Pull requests as a triage surface
17: 
18: **PRs as a request surface: no.** _(Set to `yes` if this repo treats external PRs as feature requests; `/triage` reads this flag.)_
19: 
20: When set to `yes`, PRs run through the same labels and states as issues, using the `gh pr` equivalents:
21: 
22: - **Read a PR**: `gh pr view <number> --comments` and `gh pr diff <number>` for the diff.
23: - **List external PRs for triage**: `gh pr list --state open --json number,title,body,labels,author,authorAssociation,comments` then keep only `authorAssociation` of `CONTRIBUTOR`, `FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR`, or `NONE` (drop `OWNER`/`MEMBER`/`COLLABORATOR`).
24: - **Comment / label / close**: `gh pr comment`, `gh pr edit --add-label`/`--remove-label`, `gh pr close`.
25: 
26: GitHub shares one number space across issues and PRs, so a bare `#42` may be either — resolve with `gh pr view 42` and fall back to `gh issue view 42`.
27: 
28: ## When a skill says "publish to the issue tracker"
29: 
30: Create a GitHub issue.
31: 
32: ## When a skill says "fetch the relevant ticket"
33: 
34: Run `gh issue view <number> --comments`.

(End of file - total 34 lines)
</content>

348:             .build_message(&email_account)?
349:             .to(canceled_booking.email.parse()?)
350:             .bcc(email_account.mailbox()?)
351:             .subject(subject)
352:             .singlepart(SinglePart::plain(body))?,
353:     );
354: 
355:     // create booking confirmation email for the new booking
356:     if let Some((new_booking, payment_id)) = waiting_list_booking {
357:         let subject = format!("{} Bestätigung Buchung", event.subject_prefix());
358:         let body = template::render_booking(
359:             &event.booking_template,
360:             &new_booking,
361:             &event,
362:             Some(payment_id),
363:             None,
364:             Some(false),
365:         )?;
366: 
367:         messages.push(
368:             email_sender
369:                 .build_message(&email_account)?
370:                 .to(new_booking.email.parse()?)
371:                 .bcc(email_account.mailbox()?)
372:                 .subject(subject)
373:                 .singlepart(SinglePart::plain(body))?,
374:         );
375:     }
376: 
377:     email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
378: 
379:     Ok(())

382: pub(crate) async fn send_event_email(
383:     pool: &PgPool,
384:     data: EventEmail,
385:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
386: ) -> Result<()> {
387:     if !data.bookings && !data.waiting_list {
388:         bail!("Either bookings or waiting list option need to be selected to send an event email.")
389:     }
390:     let enrolled = if data.bookings && !data.waiting_list {
391:         Some(true)
392:     } else if data.waiting_list && !data.bookings {
393:         Some(false)
394:     } else {
395:         None
396:     };
397: 
398:     let bookings = db::get_bookings(pool, &data.event_id, enrolled).await?;
399:     if bookings.is_empty() {
400:         return Ok(());
401:     }
402: 
403:     // calculate correct event id
404:     let event_id = match &data.prebooking_event_id {
405:         Some(event_id) => event_id,
406:         None => &data.event_id,
407:     };
408: 
409:     let event = db::get_event(pool, event_id, false)
410:         .await?
411:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Found no event with id '{}'", event_id))?;
412: 
413:     let email_account = event.get_associated_email_account(email_sender).await?;
414:     let message_type: MessageType = event.event_type.into();
415:     let mut messages = Vec::new();
416: 
417:     for (booking, subscriber_id, payment_id) in bookings {
418:         let prebooking_link;
419:         if let Some(event_id) = data.prebooking_event_id {
420:             prebooking_link = Some(create_prebooking_link(
421:                 event.event_type,
422:                 event_id,
423:                 subscriber_id,
424:             )?);
425:         } else {
426:             prebooking_link = None;
427:         }
428: 
429:         let body = template::render_booking(
430:             &data.body,
431:             &booking,
432:             &event,
433:             Some(payment_id),
434:             prebooking_link,
435:             None,
436:         )?;
437: 
438:         let attachments = data
439:             .attachments
440:             .as_ref()
441:             .map(|attachments| attachments.to_vec());
442: 
443:         messages.push(
444:             Email::new(
445:                 message_type,
446:                 booking.email,
447:                 data.subject.clone(),
448:                 body,
449:                 attachments,
450:             )
451:             .into_message(&email_account, email_sender)?,
452:         );
453:     }
454: 
455:     email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
456: 
457:     Ok(())
458: }

460: /// send a reminder email for each events that starts next week
461: pub(crate) async fn send_event_reminders(
462:     pool: &PgPool,
463:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
464: ) -> Result<usize> {
465:     // get all events where a event reminder should be send to the subscribers
466:     let events = db::get_reminder_events(pool).await?;
467: 
468:     // process each event
469:     for event in &events {
470:         // prepare for message generation
471:         let email_account = event.get_associated_email_account(email_sender).await?;
472:         let message_type: MessageType = event.event_type.into();
473:         let mut messages = Vec::new();
474: 
475:         // get the subject and body (depending on the event type)
476:         let (subject, body) = match event.event_type {
477:             EventType::Fitness => (
478:                 format!("{} Info zum Kursstart", event.subject_prefix()),
479:                 include_str!("../../templates/event_reminder_fitness.txt"),
480:             ),
481:             EventType::Events => (
482:                 format!("{} Info zum Eventstart", event.subject_prefix()),
483:                 include_str!("../../templates/event_reminder_events.txt"),
484:             ),
485:         };
486: 
487:         // iterate all enrolled event subscribers (Option should never be None)
488:         if let Some(subscribers) = &event.subscribers {
489:             for subscriber in subscribers.iter().filter(|s| s.enrolled) {
490:                 // render the body for the email...
491:                 let body = template::render_event_reminder(body, event, subscriber)?;
492: 
493:                 // ...and push the email into the messages list
494:                 messages.push(
495:                     Email::new(
496:                         message_type,
497:                         subscriber.email.clone(),
498:                         subject.clone(),
499:                         body,
500:                         None,
501:                     )
502:                     .into_message(&email_account, email_sender)?,
503:                 );
504:             }
505: 
506:             // send reminder emails to all event subribers
507:             email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
508: 
509:             // mark reminder has been sent to the event
510:             // (to avoid duplicate sending of reminder emails)
511:             db::mark_as_reminder_sent(pool, &event.id).await?;
512:         }
513:     }
514: 
515:     // return the count of events for which the reminder has been send
516:     Ok(events.len())

521:     pool: &PgPool,
522:     event_type: EventType,
523:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
524: ) -> Result<usize> {
525:     // get unpaid bookings and filter for bookings that are due with payment
526:     let bookings = get_unpaid_bookings(pool, event_type)
527:         .await?
528:         .into_iter()
529:         .filter(|booking| matches!(booking.due_in_days, Some(due_in_days) if due_in_days < 0))
530:         .collect::<Vec<_>>();
531: 
532:     // prepare for message generation
533:     let email_account = email_sender
534:         .account(AccountLookup::ByType(event_type.into()))
535:         .await?;
536:     let message_type: MessageType = event_type.into();
537:     let mut messages = Vec::new();
538: 
539:     // get the subject and body (depending on the event type)
540:     let subject = format!("{} Zahlungserinnerung", event_type.subject_prefix());
541:     let body = match event_type {
542:         EventType::Fitness => include_str!("../../templates/payment_reminder_fitness.txt"),

546:     let mut event_cache = HashMap::new();
547:     for booking in bookings.iter() {
548:         // get the event from the cache of from the database

565:             Email::new(
566:                 message_type,

569:                 body,
570:                 None,

576:     // send reminder emails to all bookings due with payment

579:     // mark payment reminder has been sent to the bookings

581:     let booking_ids = bookings

583:         .map(|booking| booking.booking_id)
584:         .collect::<Vec<_>>();

590: /// Check that an event has finished. If so, move the event to status 'Finished',
591: /// send the attendee confirmation email, and move the event to status 'Closed'.
592: pub(crate) async fn close_finished_running_events(
593:     pool: &PgPool,
594:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
595: ) -> Result<usize> {
596:     let mut count = 0;
597: 
598:     for event_id in db::get_all_finished_event_ids(pool).await? {
599:         // move event into status finsihed
600:         let event = update(
601:             pool,
602:             PartialEvent {
603:                 id: Some(event_id),
604:                 lifecycle_status: Some(LifecycleStatus::Finished),
605:                 ..Default::default()
606:             },
607:             email_sender,
608:         )
609:         .await?;
610: 
611:         // send confirmation emails for fitness events
612:         if matches!(event.event_type, EventType::Fitness) {
613:             send_participation_confirmation(pool, event_id, email_sender).await?;
614:         }
615: 
616:         // move event into status closed
617:         update(
618:             pool,
619:             PartialEvent {
620:                 id: Some(event_id),
621:                 lifecycle_status: Some(LifecycleStatus::Closed),
622:                 ..Default::default()
623:             },
624:             email_sender,
625:         )
626:         .await?;
627: 
628:         count += 1;
629:     }
630: 
631:     Ok(count)
632: }

642: async fn book_event(
643:     pool: &PgPool,
644:     mut booking: EventBooking,
645:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
646: ) -> Result<BookingResponse> {
647:     let event = db::get_event(pool, &booking.event_id, false)
648:         .await?
649:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event not found"))?;
650: 
651:     if event.payment_method == PaymentMethod::SepaDirectDebit {
652:         let raw_iban = booking.iban.as_ref().ok_or_else(|| {
653:             warn!(
654:                 "IBAN missing for SEPA booking of event {}",
655:                 booking.event_id
656:             );
657:             ValidationError::new("Bitte gib eine gültige IBAN ein.")
658:         })?;
659:         let normalized = banking::validate_iban_str(raw_iban)?;
660:         booking.iban = Some(normalized);
661:     } else {
662:         booking.iban = None;
663:     }
664: 
665:     let price_multiplier = event.price_relevant_multiplier(&booking.custom_values);
666:     if event.custom_fields.iter().any(|cf| cf.price_relevant) && price_multiplier.is_none() {
667:         bail!(ValidationError::new(
668:             "Bitte gib eine gültige Anzahl ein.".to_string()
669:         ));
670:     }
671: 
672:     let booking_result = db::book_event(pool, &booking).await?;
673:     let booking_response = match booking_result {
674:         BookingResult::Booked(event, counter, payment_id) => {
675:             process_booking(
676:                 pool,
677:                 &booking,
678:                 event,
679:                 counter,
680:                 true,
681:                 payment_id,
682:                 email_sender,
683:             )
684:             .await?
685:         }
686:         BookingResult::WaitingList(event, counter, payment_id) => {
687:             process_booking(
688:                 pool,
689:                 &booking,
690:                 event,
691:                 counter,
692:                 false,
693:                 payment_id,
694:                 email_sender,
695:             )
696:             .await?
697:         }
698:         BookingResult::DuplicateBooking => {
699:             info!(
700:                 "Event ({}) booking failed because a duplicate booking has been detected.",
701:                 booking.event_id
702:             );
703:             BookingResponse::failure(
704:                 "Wir haben schon eine Buchung mit diesen Anmeldedaten erkannt. Bitte verwende für weitere Buchungen andere Anmeldedaten.",
705:             )
706:         }
707:         BookingResult::NotBookable => {
708:             info!(
709:                 "Event ({}) booking failed because the event is in a unbookable state.",
710:                 booking.event_id
711:             );
712:             BookingResponse::failure("Das Event kann aktuell leider nicht gebucht werden.")
713:         }
714:         BookingResult::BookedOut => {
715:             info!(
716:                 "Booking failed because Event ({}) was overbooked.",
717:                 booking.event_id
718:             );
719:             BookingResponse::failure(MESSAGE_FAIL)
720:         }
721:     };
722: 
723:     Ok(booking_response)
724: }

726: async fn pre_book_event(
727:     pool: &PgPool,
728:     hash: String,
729:     provided_iban: Option<String>,
730:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
731: ) -> Result<BookingResponse> {
732:     let ids = hashids::decode(&hash)
733:         .with_context(|| format!("Error decoding the prebooking hash {} into ids", hash))?;
734: 
735:     if ids.len() != 2 {
736:         bail!(
737:             "Booking failed because decoded prebooking hash ({}) has an invalid length: {}",
738:             hash,
739:             ids.len()
740:         );
741:     }
742: 
743:     let event_id = EventId::from(i32::try_from(ids[0])?);
744:     let subscriber_id: i32 = ids[1].try_into()?;
745: 
746:     let event = db::get_event(pool, &event_id, false)
747:         .await?
748:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event not found"))?;
749: 
750:     if !event.lifecycle_status.is_bookable() {
751:         return Ok(BookingResponse::failure(
752:             "Diese Veranstaltung ist aktuell nicht buchbar.",
753:         ));
754:     }
755: 
756:     let mut iban = provided_iban;
757:     if event.payment_method == PaymentMethod::SepaDirectDebit && iban.is_none() {
758:         let prior_iban = db::find_prior_sepa_iban(pool, subscriber_id).await?;
759:         if let Some(prior) = prior_iban {
760:             iban = Some(prior);
761:         } else {
762:             return Ok(BookingResponse::requires_iban("Bitte gib deine IBAN ein."));
763:         }
764:     }
765: 
766:     let (booking_result, booking) = db::pre_book_event(pool, event_id, subscriber_id, iban).await?;
767:     let booking_response = match booking_result {
768:         BookingResult::Booked(event, counter, payment_id) => {
769:             process_booking(
770:                 pool,
771:                 &booking
772:                     .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Found no 'virtual' booking. Never should be here."))?,
773:                 event,
774:                 counter,
775:                 true,
776:                 payment_id,
777:                 email_sender,
778:             )
779:             .await?
780:         }
781:         BookingResult::WaitingList(event, counter, payment_id) => {
782:             process_booking(
783:                 pool,
784:                 &booking
785:                     .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Found no 'virtual' booking. Never should be here."))?,
786:                 event,
787:                 counter,
788:                 false,
789:                 payment_id,
790:                 email_sender,
791:             )
792:             .await?
793:         }
794:         BookingResult::DuplicateBooking => {
795:             warn!(
796:                 "Prebooking link data has been detected and invalidated for booking {:?}",
797:                 booking
798:             );
799:             BookingResponse::failure("Der Buchungslink wurde schon benutzt und ist daher ungültig.")
800:         }
801:         BookingResult::NotBookable => {
802:             warn!(
803:                 "Prebooking is not possible because the event is in a unbookable state for booking {:?}",
804:                 booking
805:             );
806:             BookingResponse::failure("Das Event kann aktuell leider nicht gebucht werden.")
807:         }
808:         BookingResult::BookedOut => {
809:             BookingResponse::failure("Das Event ist leider schon ausgebucht.")
810:         }
811:     };
812: 
813:     Ok(booking_response)
814: }

950: fn compare_payment_records_with_bookings(
951:     payment_records: &Vec<PaymentRecord>,
952:     bookings: &mut [VerifyPaymentBookingRecord],
953: ) -> Result<(HashMap<i32, String>, Vec<VerifyPaymentResult>)> {
954:     let mut verified_payment_bookings = Vec::new();
955:     let mut verified_ibans = HashMap::new();
956:     let mut payment_bookings_with_errors = BTreeMap::new();
957:     let mut non_matching_payment_records = Vec::new();
958: 
959:     let mut bookings = bookings
960:         .iter()
961:         .map(|booking| (&booking.payment_id, booking))
962:         .collect::<HashMap<_, _>>();
963: 
964:     for payment_record in payment_records {
965:         if payment_record.payment_ids.is_empty() {
966:             non_matching_payment_records.push(format!(
967:                 "{} / {} / {} / {}",
968:                 payment_record.payee,
969:                 payment_record.payee_iban,
970:                 payment_record.purpose,
971:                 payment_record.volumne.to_euro()
972:             ));
973:             continue;
974:         }
975: 
976:         // Try to match each payment_id individually
977:         let mut matched_bookings = Vec::new();
978:         let mut missing_ids = Vec::new();
979:         for payment_id in &payment_record.payment_ids {
980:             if let Some(booking) = bookings.remove(payment_id) {
981:                 matched_bookings.push((payment_id, booking));
982:             } else {
983:                 missing_ids.push(payment_id.to_owned());
984:             }
985:         }
986: 
987:         // Report missing IDs, but do not skip processing matched ones
988:         if !missing_ids.is_empty() {
989:             non_matching_payment_records.push(format!(
990:                 "Nicht erkannte Buchung(en): {} / {} / {} / {} / fehlende ID(s): {}",
991:                 payment_record.payee,
992:                 payment_record.payee_iban,
993:                 payment_record.purpose,
994:                 payment_record.volumne.to_euro(),
995:                 missing_ids.join(", ")
996:             ));

998: 
999:         // Check for errors in individual bookings and collect valid ones
1000:         let mut error_ids = HashSet::new();
1001:         let mut error_total = false;
1002:         for (payment_id, booking) in &matched_bookings {
1003:             if booking.payment_confirmed_at.is_some() {
1004:                 payment_bookings_with_errors
1005:                     .entry(payment_id.to_owned())

1007:                     .push("Doppelt bezahlt: Buchung ist schon als bezahlt markiert".into());
1008:                 error_ids.insert(payment_id.to_owned());

1010:             if booking.enrolled && booking.canceled.is_some() {
1011:                 payment_bookings_with_errors
1012:                     .entry(payment_id.to_owned())
1013:                     .or_insert_with(Vec::new)
1014:                     .push("Falsch bezahlt: Buchung ist als storniert markiert".into());
1015:                 error_ids.insert(payment_id.to_owned());
1016:             }
1017:             if !booking.enrolled {
1018:                 payment_bookings_with_errors
1019:                     .entry(payment_id.to_owned())
1020:                     .or_insert_with(Vec::new)
1021:                     .push("Falsch bezahlt: Buchung ist von auf der Warteliste".into());
1022:                 error_ids.insert(payment_id.to_owned());
1023:             }
1024:         }
1025: 
1026:         // Check if sum of booking prices matches payment_record.volumne
1027:         let total_booking_price = matched_bookings
1028:             .iter()
1029:             .map(|(_, b)| b.price.clone())
1030:             .fold(bigdecimal::BigDecimal::from(0), |acc, x| acc + x);
1031:         let record_volumne = payment_record.volumne.clone();
1032:         if total_booking_price.clone() != record_volumne.clone() {
1033:             for (payment_id, _) in &matched_bookings {
1034:                 payment_bookings_with_errors
1035:                     .entry(payment_id.to_owned())
1036:                     .or_insert_with(Vec::new)

1038:                         "Betrag falsch: erwartet {} != überwiesen {}",

1052:             }
1053:         }
1054:     }
1055:     verified_payment_bookings.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| a.payment_id.cmp(&b.payment_id));
1056: 
1057:     let mut compare_result = Vec::new();
1058: 
1059:     // first group are the successfull matches
1060:     compare_result.push(VerifyPaymentResult::new(
1061:         format!(

1110: /// Calculate the payday with the first event date.
1111: pub(super) fn calculate_payday(
1112:     booking_date: &DateTime<Utc>,
1113:     first_event_date: &DateTime<Utc>,
1114:     custom_days: Option<i64>,
1115: ) -> Result<NaiveDate> {
1116:     // default value is 14 days
1117:     let mut days = 14;
1118: 
1119:     // overwrite with custom days - if available
1120:     if let Some(custom_days) = custom_days {
1121:         days = custom_days;
1122:     }
1123: 
1124:     // calculated payday
1125:     let mut payday = first_event_date.date_naive()
1126:         - Duration::try_days(days)
1127:             .with_context(|| format!("Cannot create duration of {days} days."))?;
1128: 
1129:     // override the payday if it is before the day after the booking date
1130:     let earliest_paypay = booking_date.date_naive()
1131:         + Duration::try_days(1).with_context(|| "Cannot create duration of 1 day.")?;
1132:     if payday < earliest_paypay {
1133:         payday = earliest_paypay
1134:     }
1135: 
1136:     Ok(payday)
1137: }

1139: /// send participation confirmation after finished event
1140: pub(crate) async fn send_participation_confirmation(
1141:     pool: &PgPool,
1142:     event_id: EventId,
1143:     email_sender: &impl EmailGateway,
1144: ) -> Result<usize> {
1145:     // fetch the event with all subscribers
1146:     let mut event = db::get_event(pool, &event_id, true)
1147:         .await?
1148:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Error fetching event with id '{}'", event_id.get_ref()))?;
1149:     let subscribers = event.subscribers.take().ok_or_else(|| {
1150:         anyhow!(
1151:             "Subscribers of event with id '{}' are missing",
1152:             event_id.get_ref()
1153:         )
1154:     })?;
1155: 
1156:     let template = match event.event_type {
1157:         EventType::Fitness => Ok(include_str!(
1158:             "../../templates/participation_confirmation_fitness.txt"
1159:         )),
1160:         EventType::Events => Err(anyhow!(
1161:             "Participation confirmation is not supported for event type 'Events'."
1162:         )),
1163:     }?;
1164: 
1165:     let dates_len = event.dates.len();
1166:     // abort if the event has no dates
1167:     if dates_len < 1 {
1168:         return Err(anyhow!(
1169:             "Participation confirmation is not supported for an event without dates."
1170:         ));
1171:     }
1172: 
1173:     let fmt = "%d. %B %Y";
1174:     let first_date = event
1175:         .dates
1176:         .first()
1177:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event with id '{}' has no first date", event_id))?
1178:         .format_localized(fmt, Locale::de_DE)
1179:         .to_string();
1180:     let last_date = event
1181:         .dates
1182:         .last()
1183:         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Event with id '{}' has no last date", event_id))?
1184:         .format_localized(fmt, Locale::de_DE)
1185:         .to_string();
1186:     let dates = format!("{dates_len} x {} Minuten", event.duration_in_minutes);
1187: 
1188:     // send an email per participant
1189:     let email_account = event.get_associated_email_account(email_sender).await?;
1190:     let subject = format!("{} Teilnahmebestätigung", event.subject_prefix());
1191:     let mut messages = Vec::new();
1192:     for subscriber in subscribers {
1193:         if subscriber.enrolled {
1194:             let price = subscriber.total_price(&event).to_euro();
1195: 
1196:             let bytes = export::create_participation_confirmation(
1197:                 subscriber.first_name.clone(),
1198:                 subscriber.last_name.clone(),
1199:                 event.name.clone(),
1200:                 first_date.clone(),
1201:                 last_date.clone(),
1202:                 price,
1203:                 dates.clone(),
1204:             )
1205:             .await?;
1206: 
1207:             let body = template::render_participation_confirmation(template, &event, &subscriber)?;
1208: 
1209:             let message = email_sender
1210:                 .build_message(&email_account)?
1211:                 .to(subscriber.email.parse()?)
1212:                 .subject(subject.clone())
1213:                 .multipart(
1214:                     MultiPart::mixed()
1215:                         .singlepart(SinglePart::plain(body))
1216:                         .singlepart(
1217:                             Attachment::new(String::from("Teilnahmebestätigung.pdf"))
1218:                                 .body(bytes, ContentType::parse("application/pdf")?),
1219:                         ),
1220:                 )?;
1221: 
1222:             messages.push(message)
1223:         }
1224:     }
1225: 
1226:     let count = messages.len();
1227:     if count > 0 {
1228:         email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
1229:     }
1230: 
1231:     Ok(count)
1232: }

62: /// Deep email seam: every outbound email in the codebase goes through this
63: /// trait. It owns the three operations a caller needs — sending a batch of
64: /// pre-built messages, building a message rooted at a sender account, and
65: /// resolving the sender account itself — so that `new_message_builder` and
66: /// `mailbox` can stay private to this module.
67: #[async_trait]
68: #[cfg_attr(test, automock)]
69: pub trait EmailGateway {
70:     /// Send `messages` from `from`, batching over a single SMTP connection.
71:     async fn send_messages(&self, from: &EmailAccount, messages: Vec<Message>) -> Result<()>;
72: 
73:     /// Start building a [`Message`] rooted at `account` (sets `from` and the
74:     /// current date). Callers chain `.to()`, `.subject()`, `.singlepart()`,
75:     /// etc. and finalize with `?` on the resulting `Message`.
76:     fn build_message(&self, account: &EmailAccount) -> Result<MessageBuilder>;
77: 
78:     /// Resolve the [`EmailAccount`] for a given lookup (by type or by
79:     /// explicit address, e.g. an event's `alt_email_address`).
80:     async fn account(&self, lookup: AccountLookup) -> Result<EmailAccount>;
81: }

100:     /// only — not part of [`EmailGateway`] because it has no business logic
101:     /// and never needs mocking beyond the connectivity task wrapper.
102:     pub(crate) async fn test_connection(&self) -> Result<()> {
103:         let mut errors = Vec::new();

395:         None

<skill_content name="code-review">
# Skill: code-review

Two-axis review of the diff between `HEAD` and a fixed point the user supplies:

- **Standards** — does the code conform to this repo's documented coding standards?
- **Spec** — does the code faithfully implement the originating issue / PRD / spec?

Both axes run as **parallel sub-agents** so they don't pollute each other's context, then this skill aggregates their findings.

The issue tracker should have been provided to you — run `/setup-matt-pocock-skills` if `docs/agents/issue-tracker.md` is missing.

## Process

### 1. Pin the fixed point

Whatever the user said is the fixed point — a commit SHA, branch name, tag, `main`, `HEAD~5`, etc. If they didn't specify one, ask for it.

Capture the diff command once: `git diff <fixed-point>...HEAD` (three-dot, so the comparison is against the merge-base). Also note the list of commits via `git log <fixed-point>..HEAD --oneline`.

Before going further, confirm the fixed point resolves (`git rev-parse <fixed-point>`) and the diff is non-empty. A bad ref or empty diff should fail here — not inside two parallel sub-agents.

### 2. Identify the spec source

Look for the originating spec, in this order:

1. Issue references in the commit messages (`#123`, `Closes #45`, GitLab `!67`, etc.) — fetch via the workflow in `docs/agents/issue-tracker.md`.
2. A path the user passed as an argument.
3. A PRD/spec file under `docs/`, `specs/`, or `.scratch/` matching the branch name or feature.
4. If nothing is found, ask the user where the spec is. If they say there isn't one, the **Spec** sub-agent will skip and report "no spec available".

### 3. Identify the standards sources

Anything in the repo that documents how code should be written, such as `CODING_STANDARDS.md` or `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

On top of whatever the repo documents, the Standards axis always carries the **smell baseline** below — a fixed set of Fowler code smells (_Refactoring_, ch.3) that applies even when a repo documents nothing. Two rules bind it:

- **The repo overrides.** A documented repo standard always wins; where it endorses something the baseline would flag, suppress the smell.
- **Always a judgement call.** Each smell is a labelled heuristic ("possible Feature Envy"), never a hard violation — and, like any standard here, skip anything tooling already enforces.

Each smell reads *what it is* → *how to fix*; match it against the diff:

- **Mysterious Name** — a function, variable, or type whose name doesn't reveal what it does or holds. → rename it; if no honest name comes, the design's murky.
- **Duplicated Code** — the same logic shape appears in more than one hunk or file in the change. → extract the shared shape, call it from both.
- **Feature Envy** — a method that reaches into another object's data more than its own. → move the method onto the data it envies.
- **Data Clumps** — the same few fields or params keep travelling together (a type wanting to be born). → bundle them into one type, pass that.
- **Primitive Obsession** — a primitive or string standing in for a domain concept that deserves its own type. → give the concept its own small type.
- **Repeated Switches** — the same `switch`/`if`-cascade on the same type recurs across the change. → replace with polymorphism, or one map both sites share.
- **Shotgun Surgery** — one logical change forces scattered edits across many files in the diff. → gather what changes together into one module.
- **Divergent Change** — one file or module is edited for several unrelated reasons. → split so each module changes for one reason.
- **Speculative Generality** — abstraction, parameters, or hooks added for needs the spec doesn't have. → delete it; inline back until a real need shows.
- **Message Chains** — long `a.b().c().d()` navigation the caller shouldn't depend on. → hide the walk behind one method on the first object.
- **Middle Man** — a class or function that mostly just delegates onward. → cut it, call the real target direct.
- **Refused Bequest** — a subclass or implementer that ignores or overrides most of what it inherits. → drop the inheritance, use composition.

### 4. Spawn both sub-agents in parallel

Send a single message with two `Agent` tool calls. Use the `general-purpose` subagent for both.
